title: ext_type: identifier@1
description: Extension block for unique-identifier formats (UUID family, ULID, Snowflake, KSUID, NanoID, ...). Captures the citable spec, a real example value, and — where the format has a fixed internal structure — a bit-level field breakdown, plus whether instances sort lexicographically/chronologically. Byte/bit values are described, not raw control bytes; any non-printable bytes in strings use JSON escapes per the rest of the corpus.

spec.description: The defining specification, human-readable (e.g. 'RFC 9562', 'ULID spec (github.com/ulid/spec)', 'Twitter Snowflake', 'KSUID (github.com/segmentio/ksuid)'). The citable URL lives in core source_url / attribution.

example.description: A real, valid example identifier in its canonical string form (e.g. a v4 UUID, a 26-char ULID, a snowflake decimal). Chosen so it actually parses to the documented version/variant where applicable.

bit_layout.description: Ordered, most-significant-first breakdown of the identifier's fixed binary structure. Omitted for formats with no fixed internal layout (e.g. random NanoID).

bits.description: Width of this field in bits.

meaning.description: What the field encodes, including any fixed value (e.g. 'version = 0b0111 (7)', 'variant = 0b10').

length_bits.description: Total size of the identifier in bits (e.g. 128 for UUID/ULID, 64 for Snowflake, 160 for KSUID). For text-only formats with no fixed binary width (NanoID), give the typical entropy or omit.

sortable.description: True if instances sort lexicographically in (roughly) creation-time order in their canonical string form (UUIDv7, ULID, Snowflake, KSUID = true; UUIDv4, NanoID = false).

encoding.description: Canonical string encoding of the identifier (e.g. 'hex with dashes (8-4-4-4-12)', "Crockford's Base32", 'base62', 'decimal uint64', 'URL-safe alphabet A-Za-z0-9_-').

notes.description: Optional clarifications/gotchas (epoch offsets, monotonicity within a millisecond, variant bits, etc.). One item per note.
